checks.housingWrong: The housing rectangle is either in the wrong place or the wrong size. Check `housingX`, `housingY`, `housingWidth`, and `housingHeight`.
checks.redWrong: The red light is either in the wrong place or the wrong size. Check `redY`.
checks.yellowWrong: The yellow light is either in the wrong place or the wrong size. Check `yellowY`.
checks.greenWrong: The green light is either in the wrong place or the wrong size. Check `greenY`.
checks.doesntScale: Your code doesn't scale correctly when `radius` is {{radius}}. Every position and size should be derived from `center` and `radius`.

buildRelationalTrafficLights.name: Build the relational traffic lights
buildRelationalTrafficLights.description: Derive every position and size from `center` and `radius` so the traffic light scales correctly.

buildRelationalTrafficLights.name: Build the relational traffic lights
buildRelationalTrafficLights.description: Derive every position and size from `center` and `radius`.

howToWorkOutYellowCenter.question: How do I work out the yellow light's center?
howToWorkOutYellowCenter.answer: You have a variable for `center` provided to you. You can set `yellowY` to equal it.

howToWorkOutRedCenter.question: How do I work out the center of the red light?
howToWorkOutRedCenter.answer: If the two circles are touching, how far apart are their centers? You can subtract this from `center` to get the red light.

whereHousingStarts.question: Where does the housing rectangle start?
whereHousingStarts.answer: If you know the center of the red circle, you can calculate the top of the red circle. The housing starts 1 radius above (before) the top of the red circle. Similarly you can calculate the left of a light and then go 1 radius further for the start.

howBigHousing.question: How big should the housing be?
howBigHousing.answer: It's the width of a light, plus padding of 1 radius either side. Height is 2 radii per light plus a radius top and bottom for padding.

whyMultipleOfSomething.question: Why must everything be a multiple of something else?
whyMultipleOfSomething.answer: If you hardcode a number, changing `radius` will leave the housing the wrong size for the lights. Using formulas involving `radius` and `center` keep the whole drawing in proportion.

rectangle.description: Draw a rectangle at position (left, top) with the given width, height, and color

circle.description: Draw a circle centered at (centerX, centerY) with the given radius and color
